meta.name: Saikat Bandyopadhyay
meta.title: Full-Stack Biomedical Data Scientist & Clinical Pipeline Architect
meta.tagline: Decoding the language of life to end human suffering

about.bio: I am a data scientist focused on building next-generation data solutions at the intersection of disease risk and health. Increasing data volume across several domains requires scalable, robust, auditable data pipelines and processes to best use their data in the service of generating business intelligence. My skill is in systems design and complex systems architecture in terms of data-driven decision making.

highlights.label: Clinical-Grade
highlights.value: CLIA/CAP + HIPAA/SOC 2 compliant pipeline design with automated validation gates and audit trails

highlights.label: Real-Time QC
highlights.value: Early warning systems with sequencing quality dashboards and anomaly detection

highlights.label: Scalable Architecture
highlights.value: Modular, fault-tolerant pipelines — containerized, checkpointed, horizontally scalable on HPC

highlights.label: Stack
highlights.value: Python, R, PyTorch, Nextflow, Snakemake, DRAGEN, Docker, SLURM

projects.title: RAPID-CT: AI-Powered Brain Hemorrhage Detection
projects.description: Deep learning framework detecting Intracranial Hemorrhage from brain CT scans using segmentation-based ResNet architecture. Engineered a PII-blind REST API with split-key encryption for clinical deployment — because no patient should wait for a diagnosis while their data waits for permission.
projects.outcome: High-precision ICH classification with privacy-preserving clinical deployment

projects.title: Causal Gene Discovery Pipeline
projects.description: Computational colocalization pipeline integrating GWAS and bulk RNA-seq eQTL data to identify candidate causal genes in Diversity Outbred mice. Implemented conditional analysis with bootstrapping to validate concordance between conditional and colocalization signals.
projects.outcome: Identified novel causal gene candidates at significantly associated loci

projects.title: Proteogenomics: Beyond the Reference Genome
projects.description: Nextflow-based pipeline integrating long-read RNA sequencing with mass spectrometry to discover novel transcript isoforms and protein products absent from GENCODE annotations. Rewriting what we thought we knew about the proteome.
projects.outcome: Identified novel protein isoforms absent from GENCODE reference

projects.title: Single-Cell Trajectory Analysis of Bone Marrow
projects.description: Pseudotime trajectory analysis on single-cell RNA-seq data characterizing gene expression dynamics along osteoblast and osteoclast differentiation in bone marrow stromal cells — mapping the molecular choreography of bone formation.
projects.outcome: Mapped differentiation trajectories and prioritized causal genes via network hub overlap

projects.title: Low-Pass WGS Processing Pipeline
projects.description: High-throughput processing pipeline for Low-Pass Whole Genome Sequencing achieving 15% reduction in genotyping costs while maintaining fidelity for downstream haplotype calling and eQTL analysis.
projects.outcome: 15% reduction in genotyping costs with preserved data fidelity

projects.title: FAIR Data Governance Framework
projects.description: Architected a standardized data governance framework enforcing FAIR principles across all research data assets — transforming chaos into a reproducible, audit-ready ecosystem.
projects.outcome: Transformed unstructured research environment into reproducible ecosystem

experience.company: University of Virginia — Dept. of Genome Sciences
bullets: Discovered 2 novel genes associated with bone strength by designing a scalable colocalization pipeline integrating Diversity Outbred mouse and human GWAS/eQTL data with conditional analysis and bootstrapping validation; leading manuscript preparation

Reduced HPC batch runtime by 50% (48h → 24h) for 200-sample genome reconstruction jobs by implementing vectorized data object handling and optimized SLURM thread allocation, accelerating iterative analysis cycles

Prioritized candidate genes for experimental validation by constructing modular WGCNA modules with Bayesian network hub gene identification, engineered with checkpoint-based fault tolerance for multi-day compute jobs

experience.company: University of Virginia — Dept. of Genome Sciences
bullets: Saved 60% per-sample genotyping cost (~$100 → ~$40) across 1,000+ samples by building an end-to-end LP-WGS processing pipeline, preserving fidelity for downstream haplotype calling and eQTL analysis
Discovered novel protein isoforms absent from GENCODE reference (published in JASMS, 2024) by deploying a Nextflow-based proteogenomics pipeline integrating long-read RNA-seq with mass spectrometry
Standardized 50+ research datasets across 20+ researchers into a reproducible, audit-ready ecosystem by architecting a FAIR data governance framework with metadata standards and ETL automation

Shipped a QC validation pipeline adopted as the organization-wide standard for all DRAGEN-based clinical assays, processing 200–1,000 patient samples/week within a CLIA/CAP-certified, HIPAA + SOC 2-compliant environment
Prevented downstream clinical errors by designing automated validation gates that flagged sequencing failures, caught anomalous runs, and reduced manual QC review turnaround time
Ensured regulatory traceability across all pipeline runs by implementing audit trail logging and redundancy/failover patterns in a highly regulated clinical genomics environment
Improved engineering team utilization by developing a resource allocation engine that optimized staff deployment across concurrent assay productization workstreams

experience.company: Governing Health Futures 2030 (The Lancet & Financial Times Commission)
bullets: Shaped digital health infrastructure strategy for a global commission co-chaired by The Lancet and Financial Times by delivering technical architecture reviews and scalability assessments
Strengthened engineering rigor of public health data systems by performing code reviews and architecture assessments that aligned infrastructure design with best practices

experience.company: CSIR-IGIB — Institute of Genomics and Integrative Biology
bullets: Achieved sub-2-minute inference latency for emergency brain hemorrhage triage by developing the RAPID-CT deep learning framework, validated on 1,000+ CT scans in partnership with a hospital collaborator
Enabled privacy-preserving clinical AI deployment by engineering a PII-blind REST API with split-key encryption, redundant inference paths, and health monitoring endpoints
Democratized cancer genomics data for 30+ researchers across multiple institutions by building automated monitoring pipelines and dashboards for TCGA/CPTAC datasets with real-time quality alerting
Mentored junior researchers and presented findings at national conferences, contributing to the institute's computational biology training pipeline
Shipped somatic mutation visualization modules integrated into Elucidata's Polly platform by engineering client-facing dashboard components for cancer genomics variant data exploration

IS-PRM-Based Peptide Targeting Informed by Long-Read Sequencing for Alternative Proteome Detection
